archetype: Real-Time Acoustic-to-Visual Beamforming & Bistatic Sonar Fusion Engine for Subsea Unexploded Ordnance (UXO) Classification
domain: Maritime Defense Engineering & Offshore Geophysical Survey
description: This system fuses data from multi-static sonar arrays and optical sensors to classify and geolocate unexploded ordnance on the seabed in turbid environments, used by ROV operators and naval survey teams. It requires real-time signal processing to disentangle multi-path acoustic reflections from actual threats while adhering to strict military-grade encryption and latency constraints.
rules: MUST implement real-time Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) and beamforming algorithms compliant with US Navy SONAR data formats (e.g., SONAR Data Interchange Format - SDIF) within 15ms latency budgets.
MUST enforce FIPS 140-2 Level 3 cryptographic standards for all data-at-rest and in-transit communication due to classification of ordnance threat data.
MUST integrate with proprietary ROV hydraulic control APIs (e.g., Kongsberg HMI or Teledyne Gavia) to trigger immediate abort or hover maneuvers upon high-probability UXO detection.
MUST handle asynchronous, bursty data streams from heterogeneous sensors (passive hydrophones, active sonar pingers, and low-light turbidity cameras) without packet loss during high-current drift.
MUST provide deterministic time-stamping synchronized to GPS-locked atomic clocks (PTP/IEEE 1588) to within 1 microsecond for accurate trilateration of submerged objects.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use standard RESTful APIs for streaming sensor data, as TCP retransmission jitter creates fatal gaps in acoustic phase analysis required for beamforming.

DO NOT rely on cloud-based batch processing for threat detection, as the system must operate in 'store-and-forward' offline mode during deep-sea operations where connectivity is intermittent or non-existent.
DO NOT apply generic object detection AI models trained on clear-water datasets, as they fail catastrophically on subsea targets obscured by sediment, bioluminescence, and acoustic shadowing.
key_technical_challenges: Compensating for real-time Doppler shift and sound speed profile (SSP) variations caused by thermoclines to prevent false negatives in sonar pinging.
Synchronizing multi-modal sensor data streams (acoustic, visual, magnetic) with sub-millisecond precision despite differing onboard clock drift under high-pressure conditions.
Optimizing matrix inversion algorithms for acoustic cross-correlation on edge hardware with limited thermal dissipation and power budgets in a ROV payload.

recommended_stack.language: Rust + one-line reason: Provides memory safety guarantees without a garbage collector, essential for real-time signal processing loops.
recommended_stack.framework: Rust + Tokio for async runtimes + one-line reason: Handles high-throughput asynchronous I/O from multiple sensor drivers with deterministic task scheduling.
recommended_stack.database_engine: TimescaleDB (PostgreSQL extension) + one-line reason: Optimized for high-frequency time-series ingestion of acoustic waveforms and geospatial metadata.
recommended_stack.cache: Redis Streams + one-line reason: Low-latency buffering for transient sensor data bursts before persistent storage or edge inference.
recommended_stack.message_broker: ZeroMQ (ZMTP) + one-line reason: Provides lock-free, high-performance message passing suitable for real-time data pipelines without serialization overhead.
special_services: Hardware-Wrapped AES-NI Encryption Module for secure telemetry
Geospatial Projection Service for WGS84 to UTM conversion under dynamic tidal models

compliance_flags: ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations) Technical Data Controls
NIST SP 800-171 Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI) Requirements
IMO (International Maritime Organization) Guidelines for Remotely Operated Vehicles
